entities.name: Mini-model orchestration
entities.context: Core architectural belief that orchestrating multiple smaller AI models creates more resilient and cost-effective systems than single frontier models

entities.name: Swiss Army Knife fallacy
entities.context: Framework arguing that one tool for everything is less effective than specialized tools; used as reasoning for modular AI architecture

entities.name: Agentic orchestration
entities.context: Implementation approach for coordinating discrete AI steps across smaller, faster models

entities.name: Prompt-chaining
entities.context: Technique for connecting multiple model calls in sequence to build sophisticated outputs

entities.name: Modularity
entities.context: Software engineering principle that reduces single points of failure and enables easier debugging and component upgrades

entities.name: Frontier model
entities.context: Single large AI model approach; contrasted as riskier due to pricing/availability dependence and commoditization risk

relationships.context: Fallacy provides reasoning basis for why orchestration beats single-model approaches
Agentic orchestration is the recommended implementation approach
Prompt-chaining is a key technique for implementing the architecture
Modularity principle from software engineering supports the orchestration approach
Positioned as superior alternative to frontier model dependence
